prompt.content: We’re trying to improve service quality and retention by identifying the single best agent who can cover the five most dissatisfied customers. Use the customer_service_interactions table to find the five customers with the lowest average satisfaction (no minimum resolved-interaction requirement). Break ties for the top-5 customers by most recent interaction date, then by subscriber_id. Compute per‑agent metrics (interaction count, average handle time, resolution rate, and average satisfaction), normalize each metric across all agents using min‑max scaling (0-1), and invert handle time so lower is better. Calculate a composite score weighted as 40% resolution rate, 30% satisfaction, 20% handle time (inverted), and 10% interaction volume. Identify agents who are eligible for all five customers, where eligibility means the agent covers all of a customer’s channels OR all of a customer’s interaction types. From those agents, pick the highest composite score (tie-break by higher resolution rate, higher satisfaction, lower handle time, then agent_id). Handle missing values by relying on SQLite defaults: AVG ignores NULLs; resolution_status NULLs are treated as not resolved; NULL channels or interaction types don’t add requirements in the eligibility checks. Report the chosen agent’s information: agent id, composite score, resolution rate, average satisfaction, and average handle time minutes, rounding all reported metrics to two decimals. Perform the analysis using SQLite. Provide the SQL script and results.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
agent_id   composite_score   resolution_rate avg_satisfaction avg_handle_time_minutes
  AG8261            0.79                        1.00                 5.00                        34.00

```sql
-- 1) Per-agent base metrics
WITH agent_base AS (
  SELECT
    agent_id,
    COUNT(*) AS interactions,
    AVG(duration_minutes) AS avg_handle_time_minutes,
    AVG(CASE WHEN LOWER(resolution_status) = 'resolved' THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS resolution_rate,
    AVG(satisfaction_score) AS avg_satisfaction
  FROM customer_service_interactions
  GROUP BY agent_id
),
-- 2) Normalize metrics to 0-1 (force float division)
agent_norm AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    (1.0 * interactions - MIN(interactions) OVER())
      / NULLIF(1.0 * (MAX(interactions) OVER() - MIN(interactions) OVER()), 0) AS n_interactions,
    (1.0 * resolution_rate - MIN(resolution_rate) OVER())
      / NULLIF(1.0 * (MAX(resolution_rate) OVER() - MIN(resolution_rate) OVER()), 0) AS n_resolution,
    (1.0 * avg_satisfaction - MIN(avg_satisfaction) OVER())
      / NULLIF(1.0 * (MAX(avg_satisfaction) OVER() - MIN(avg_satisfaction) OVER()), 0) AS n_satisfaction,
    1.0 - (
      (1.0 * avg_handle_time_minutes - MIN(avg_handle_time_minutes) OVER())
      / NULLIF(1.0 * (MAX(avg_handle_time_minutes) OVER() - MIN(avg_handle_time_minutes) OVER()), 0)
    ) AS n_handle_time
  FROM agent_base
),
-- 3) Composite score from normalized metrics
agent_scores AS (
  SELECT
    agent_id,
    resolution_rate,
    avg_satisfaction,
    avg_handle_time_minutes,
    (0.40 * n_resolution
   + 0.30 * n_satisfaction
   + 0.20 * n_handle_time
   + 0.10 * n_interactions) AS composite_score
  FROM agent_norm
),
-- 4) Per-customer satisfaction and recency
customer_stats AS (
  SELECT
    subscriber_id,
    AVG(satisfaction_score) AS avg_satisfaction,
    MAX(interaction_date) AS last_interaction_date
  FROM customer_service_interactions
  GROUP BY subscriber_id
),
-- 5) Rank customers by lowest satisfaction (tie-break by recency, id)
worst_customers AS (
  SELECT
    subscriber_id,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      ORDER BY
        avg_satisfaction ASC,
        last_interaction_date DESC,
        subscriber_id
    ) AS customer_rank
  FROM customer_stats
),
-- 6) Top 5 lowest-satisfaction customers
top_customers AS (
  SELECT
    subscriber_id,
    customer_rank
  FROM worst_customers
  WHERE customer_rank <= 5
),
-- 7) Agent eligible if full channels OR full interaction types for customer
eligible_pairs AS (
  SELECT
    c.subscriber_id,
    a.agent_id
  FROM top_customers c
  JOIN agent_scores a
    ON 1 = 1
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM (
      SELECT DISTINCT channel
      FROM customer_service_interactions
      WHERE subscriber_id = c.subscriber_id
    ) cc
    WHERE cc.channel NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT channel
      FROM customer_service_interactions
      WHERE agent_id = a.agent_id
    )
  )
  OR NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM (
      SELECT DISTINCT interaction_type
      FROM customer_service_interactions
      WHERE subscriber_id = c.subscriber_id
    ) ct
    WHERE ct.interaction_type NOT IN (
      SELECT DISTINCT interaction_type
      FROM customer_service_interactions
      WHERE agent_id = a.agent_id
    )
  )
),
-- 8) Agents eligible for all 5 customers
eligible_all AS (
  SELECT
    agent_id
  FROM eligible_pairs
  GROUP BY agent_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT subscriber_id) = 5
),
-- 9) Pick the best eligible agent by score (tie-break by resolution, satisfaction, handle time, id)
top_agent AS (
  SELECT
    s.agent_id,
    s.composite_score,
    s.resolution_rate,
    s.avg_satisfaction,
    s.avg_handle_time_minutes,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      ORDER BY
        s.composite_score DESC,
        s.resolution_rate DESC,
        s.avg_satisfaction DESC,
        s.avg_handle_time_minutes ASC,
        s.agent_id
    ) AS agent_rank
  FROM agent_scores s
  WHERE s.agent_id IN (SELECT agent_id FROM eligible_all)
)
-- 10) Report agent metrics only
SELECT
  t1.agent_id,
  printf('%.2f', t1.composite_score) AS composite_score,
  printf('%.2f', t1.resolution_rate) AS resolution_rate,
  printf('%.2f', t1.avg_satisfaction) AS avg_satisfaction,
  printf('%.2f', t1.avg_handle_time_minutes) AS avg_handle_time_minutes
FROM top_agent t1
WHERE t1.agent_rank = 1;
```
